purpose: Initial reviewed MIPVU sample for the Lincoln pilot corpus. The sample demonstrates the codebook workflow and provides a future reliability/adjudication target without representing the full corpus as complete.
selection_rationale: Includes at least one high-salience sentence from each current Lincoln pilot document.
Covers body-politic survival language, sacrificial death language, birth/renewal language, and providential moral-accounting language.
Reviews full sentences rather than isolated lexical units so non-metaphor decisions are documented beside metaphor-related decisions.

review_policy.unit_scope: all lexical units in each selected sentence
review_policy.non_metaphor_policy: Reviewed non-metaphor units receive decision_type=non_metaphor and review_status=reviewed; detailed rationale is required only for metaphor-related or uncertain decisions.
review_policy.metaphor_policy: Metaphor-related and uncertain decisions include contextual_meaning, basic_meaning, basic_meaning_source, contrast_explanation, comparison_basis, confidence, review_notes, semantic_shift_risk, and candidate domain hints.
review_policy.downstream_policy: CMT and Koenigsbergian annotation may cite only MIPVU-positive or uncertain units unless explicitly marked exploratory_without_mipvu.

sampled_sentences.rationale: Battlefield burial, giving lives, and national life.
Final dedication sequence, last full measure of devotion, new birth, and democratic survival.
Providential blood repayment, debt/accounting, lash/sword, and divine judgment.

limitations: This is not an inter-annotator reliability sample.
The remaining Lincoln lexical units are pending.
Pilot decisions should be adjudicated before publication-facing aggregate claims use them as final evidence.
